LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SPANISH HILLS SURGERY CENTER'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO TRNASFER ACTION TO VENTURA COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO MEET ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING AS TO THAT SAN FRANCISCO IS A SERIOUSLY INCONVENIENT LOCATION FOR NON-PARTY, NON-PARTY EMPLOYEE WITNESSES TO APPEAR, OR WHY THE ENDS OF JUSTICE WOULD PROMOTED BY TRANSFER TO VENTURA COUNTY. DEFENDANT PRESENTS DECLARATIONS THAT WERE PRESENTED IN ITS JULY 2012 MOTION, WHICH WAS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE, PLUS THE DECLARATION OF DEFENDANT KATOUZIAN, WHO IS A PARTY. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS EVIDENT THAT THREE NON-PARTY PHYSICIAN WITNESSES WOULD NOT BE INCONVENIENCED BY A TRIAL IN SAN FRANCISCO. FURTHER, TRIAL IN THIS CASE IS SET FOR MAY 13, 2013. THERE WOULD BE A SUBSTANTIAL TRIAL DELAY IF THE CASE WERE TRANSFERRED AT THIS LATE DATE TO VENTURA COUNTY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT BRISTOL-MYERS SQUIBB COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE GENERAL DEMURRER TO THE ENTIRE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") AND AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION; SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE GENERAL DEMURRER TO THE ENTIRE FAC, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS AMENDED THEIR COMPLAINT AND IDENTIFIED BRISTOL-MYERS SQUIBB COMPANY ("BMS") AS DOE "1" AS TO THE EXISTING FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. AT A MINIMUM THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION (WHERE BMS WAS IDENTIFIED AS DOE "1") ARE NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THUS, THE GENERAL DEMURRER TO THE ENTIRE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ON STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS GROUNDS IS OVERRULED. (SEE WEILL & BROWN, CAL. PRACTICE GUIDE: CIV. PROC. BEFORE TRIAL (RUTTER) 7:105.) NO OTHER GROUNDS FOR DEMURRER ARE ASSERTED AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF AGREES TO DISMISS THIS CAUSE OF ACTION, AND THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR STRICT PRODUCT LIABILITY/FAILURE TO WARN, PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION. (SEE CACI 1205.)AMENDED COMPLAINT TO BE FILED IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SPANISH HILLS SURGERY CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT EXIST AS TO WHETHER DR. KATOUZIAN WAS THE OSTENSIBLE AGENT OF DEFENDANT. FOR EXAMPLE, THE PARTIES DISPUTE WHETHER DR. KATOUZIAN EVER DISCLOSED TO PLAINTIFF THAT DR. KATOUZIAN WAS NOT AN AGENT OR EMPLOYEE OF DEFENDANT (PLAINTIFF'S ADDITIONAL UMF #4), AND WHETHER PLAINTIFF ONLY SAW DR. KATOUZIAN AT SPANISH HILLS SURGERY CENTER (PLAINTIFF'S ADDITIONAL UMF #3). IN ADDITION, DEFENDANT'S ATTEMPT TO DENY THAT PLAINTIFF WAS NEVER INFORMED BY ANYONE THAT DR. KATOUZIAN WAS NOT DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEE AND THAT PLAINTIFF AT ALL TIMES BELIEVED HIM TO BE DEFENDANT'S AGENT OR EMPLOYEE, NONE OF THE EVIDENCE PROFFERED IN SUPPORT CONTRADICTS THOSE ASSERTIONS. (SEE PLAINTIFF'S ADDITIONAL UMF #6). DEFENDANT POINTS TO NOTHING IN ITS EVIDENCE INDICATING THAT PLAINTIFF RECEIVED OR WAS AWARE OF EITHER THE REFERRAL FORM OR ANY OF DR. KATOUZIAN'S CONSULTATION REPORTS. FURTHERMORE, THE CONSENT FORM CONTAINS DEFENDANT'S LOGO AND ADDRESS AS A HEADER, AND IS SILENT AS TO THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN DEFENDANT AND DR. KATOUZIAN EXCEPTING THE NOTICE THAT DR. KATOUZIAN HAS A FINANCIAL INTEREST IN THE CENTER. THERE IS NO REASON TO INFER THAT THIS DISCLOSURE EXPLICITLY OR IMPLICITLY ALERTED PLAINTIFF THAT DR. KATOUZIAN WAS NOT THE EMPLOYEE OR AGENT OF DEFENDANT. ADDITIONALLY, DEFENDANT'S GENERAL REFERENCE TO OVER 200 PAGES OF MEDICAL RECORDS-WITHOUT FURTHER EXPLANATION OR CITATION-FAILS TO SUPPORT ITS DENIAL. FINALLY, DEFENDANT'S CONTENTIONS AND EVIDENCE BEARING ON WHETHER DR. KATOUZIAN WAS ACTUALLY AN AGENT OF DEFENDANT HAVE NO RELEVANCE IN DETERMINING WHETHER DEFENDANT CAN BE LIABLE ON A THEORY OF OSTENSIBLE AGENCY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST J. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANDREW J. SPIELBERGER, TEL. NO. (424)-832-7677 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARK B. CONNELY & ATTY. JENNIFER J. HENDERSON OF HH&C, TEL. NO. (805) 544-3830 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SPANISH HILLS SURGERY CENTER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Triable issues of material fact exist as to whether Dr. Katouzian was the ostensible agent of Defendant. For example, the parties dispute whether Dr. Katouzian ever disclosed to Plaintiff that Dr. Katouzian was not an agent or employee of Defendant (Plaintiff's Additional UMF #4), and whether Plaintiff only saw Dr. Katouzian at Spanish Hills Surgery Center (Plaintiff's Additional UMF #3). In addition, Defendant's attempt to deny that Plaintiff was never informed by anyone that Dr. Katouzian was not Defendant's employee and that Plaintiff at all times believed him to be Defendant's agent or employee, none of the evidence proffered in support contradicts those assertions. (See Plaintiff's Additional UMF #6). Defendant points to nothing in its evidence indicating that Plaintiff received or was aware of either the referral form or any of Dr. Katouzian's Consultation Reports. Furthermore, the Consent Form contains Defendant's logo and address as a header, and is silent as to the relationship between Defendant and Dr. Katouzian excepting the notice that Dr. Katouzian has a financial interest in the Center. There is no reason to infer that this disclosure explicitly or implicitly alerted Plaintiff that Dr. Katouzian was not the employee or agent of Defendant. Additionally, Defendant's general reference to over 200 pages of medical records-without further explanation or citation-fails to support its denial. Finally, Defendant's contentions and evidence bearing on whether Dr. Katouzian was actually an agent of Defendant have no relevance in determining whether Defendant can be liable on a theory of ostensible agency. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BRISTOL-MYERS SQUIBB COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled as to the general demurrer to the entire First Amended Complaint ("FAC") and as to the fifth cause of action; sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action. As to the general demurrer to the entire FAC, the demurrer is overruled because Plaintiffs amended their Complaint and identified Bristol-Myers Squibb Company ("BMS") as Doe "1" as to the existing first and second causes of action. At a minimum the first and second causes of action (where BMS was identified as Doe "1") are not barred by the statute of limitations. Thus, the general demurrer to the entire First Amended Complaint on statute of limitations grounds is overruled. (See Weill & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (Rutter) 7:105.) No other grounds for demurrer are asserted as to the first and second causes of action. As to the fourth cause of action, Plaintiff agrees to dismiss this cause of action, and the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. As to the fifth cause of action for strict product liability/failure to warn, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action. (See CACI 1205.)Amended Complaint to be filed in light of the ruling on the demurrer. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT SPANISH HILLS SURGERY CENTER'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Trnasfer Action To Ventura County Superior Court is denied. Defendant has failed to meet its burden of showing as to that San Francisco is a seriously inconvenient location for non-party, non-party employee witnesses to appear, or why the ends of justice would promoted by transfer to Ventura County. Defendant presents declarations that were presented in its July 2012 motion, which was denied without prejudice, plus the declaration of Defendant Katouzian, who is a party. Plaintiff presents evident that three non-party physician witnesses would not be inconvenienced by a trial in San Francisco. Further, trial in this case is set for May 13, 2013. There would be a substantial trial delay if the case were transferred at this late date to Ventura County. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J.
